uplink.description: fPort 10 GNSS fix (lat 48.1, lon 11.5, moving, 3.700V, 9 sats)
uplink._note: Synthetic in-bounds input; expected derived via the ORACLE method by running reference/upstream-codec.js (tag-s-l.js, shared with tag-l). Upstream yields latitude 48.1, longitude 11.5, altitude 523 m, batteryVoltage 3700 mV, pdop 12, moving true, conf_change_id 1. Normalized: lat/lon -> position, moving -> action.motion.detected, 3700 mV -> battery 3.7 V (status 0x09 => moving + confChangeId 1).

uplink.description: fPort 110 buffered GNSS fix (buffer level 7, steady, 4.100V)
uplink._note: Synthetic buffered GNSS input; oracle (upstream) confirms bufferLevel 7, batteryVoltage 4100 mV (=> battery 4.1 V), pdop 8, moving false. 2-byte buffer level (7) precedes the status byte (0x08 => steady).

Synthetic combined uplink; oracle (upstream) yields wifiData of two APs and wifiTimestamp 1699999968 (= gnssTimestamp - ttf + 10). 20-byte GNSS block followed by two 7-byte WiFi AP records (MAC + int8 RSSI).

uplink.description: fPort 151 buffered combined GNSS + 1 WiFi AP
uplink._note: Synthetic buffered combined uplink; oracle (upstream) confirms bufferLevel 3 and one WiFi AP. 2-byte buffer level (3) precedes the 20-byte GNSS block then one WiFi AP record.

errors: Invalid payload length for GNSS packet

uplink._note: Synthetic short payload; fewer than the 20 bytes required for a GNSS fix. Oracle (upstream) returns the same length error.

uplink.description: fPort 6 button-press uplink carries no normalized measurement
uplink._note: Synthetic; upstream decodes port 6 as a button-press event, but it carries no vocabulary measurement so this codec reports it unsupported. Only the position-bearing ports 10/110/51/151 are normalized.
